meta_title: Impact Doors in Lauderdale Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Lauderdale Lakes, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind-rated, $0 down financing. Call the Kings of Service!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Lauderdale Lakes Homes — Storm-Ready, Code-Compliant, and Built to Last
intro:
Lauderdale Lakes sits squarely in one of South Florida's most storm-active corridors, and the homes here know it. With approximately 60% of the city's housing stock built before 1994 — many in neighborhoods like Oakland Forest, Lake Emerald, and the Vincent Torres Park Area — a large portion of local properties are still protected by original hollow-core entry doors and aging sliding glass panels that were never designed to withstand today's understood threat levels. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we help Lauderdale Lakes homeowners close that gap with professionally installed, hurricane-rated impact doors that meet and exceed every current Florida Building Code requirement for Broward County.

Lauderdale Lakes may sit roughly six miles from the Atlantic coast, but don't let that distance suggest your home is out of harm's way. Broward County as a whole sits in a designated 170mph wind zone, meaning every door on your property must be engineered and rated to handle catastrophic wind pressure, wind-borne debris, and the relentless cycling of pressure differentials a major hurricane produces. The city's relatively dense residential neighborhoods — many featuring single-family homes valued around $250,000 — are lined with older wood-frame and concrete-block structures whose original door systems simply weren't built to those standards. Upgrading to impact doors isn't a luxury for Lauderdale Lakes families; it's one of the smartest structural decisions a homeowner can make.

Beyond storm protection, impact doors deliver everyday value that accumulates year over year. They reduce outside noise — a meaningful benefit for properties near busy corridors like State Road 7 or Commercial Boulevard — improve your home's energy efficiency by sealing out humid South Florida air, and provide a visible deterrent against forced entry. Whether you're a long-time resident in Lake Emerald or you've recently purchased a property near C&I Studios and you're thinking about smart upgrades, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team is ready to help you choose the right impact door solution for your home, your budget, and your lifestyle.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderdale Lakes Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing didn't earn the title 'Kings of Service' by accident. With more than 60 years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our team has worked on hundreds of homes that look and feel a lot like the ones found throughout Lauderdale Lakes — older concrete-block ranch homes, mid-century split-levels, and updated townhomes that each present their own framing and structural nuances. When you call us, you're not handing your project off to a subcontractor. Every installation is completed entirely by our in-house crew, meaning the same licensed professionals who assess your home are the ones measuring, preparing the opening, setting the frame, and finishing the job. That continuity matters enormously in a market where cut-rate companies routinely outsource labor and leave homeowners holding the bag when problems arise.

Our A+ Better Business Bureau rating and 500+ completed homes across South Florida speak to a consistent track record of doing things right the first time. We understand that for many Lauderdale Lakes families operating on a practical household budget, an impact door installation represents a significant investment. That's why we approach every project with transparency: clear pricing, honest timelines, and a genuine consultation process that helps you understand what you're getting and why. We carry all required Broward County licensing and insurance, and we handle every permit application directly with the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division so you don't have to navigate that process alone.

We also recognize that Lauderdale Lakes is a community with deep roots and neighbors who talk to each other. Our reputation in neighboring cities like Fort Lauderdale, Oakland Park, Lauderhill, and Tamarac has been built one satisfied customer at a time, and we bring that same commitment to every home in this city. From the first phone call to the final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your partner — not just your contractor. That's what separates the Kings of Service from everyone else working in this market.

section_history.h2: Lauderdale Lakes Has Faced Nine Storms Since 1990 — Here's What That Means for Your Doors
section_history.body:
Lauderdale Lakes and the surrounding Broward County communities have experienced nine notable storm events since 1990, and three of those storms left lasting marks on local memory and local property values. Hurricane Andrew tore through South Florida in August of 1992 with catastrophic force, prompting a complete overhaul of the Florida Building Code that dramatically raised the bar for how homes in this region must be constructed and protected. Many of the pre-1994 homes in Lauderdale Lakes were built before those updated codes took effect, which means their original doors — and in many cases their replacement doors — may not meet current hurricane-resistance standards.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was a different kind of threat. One of the fastest-intensifying Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, Wilma made landfall in Southwest Florida and rapidly crossed the peninsula, battering Broward County with sustained winds and the kind of wind-borne debris that exposes every weak point in a home's envelope. Doors that appeared structurally sound beforehand revealed catastrophic vulnerabilities when subjected to real storm conditions. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked up the Florida peninsula and delivered prolonged wind exposure to Lauderdale Lakes, once again testing the resilience of aging entry systems throughout the city's neighborhoods.

This documented storm history is not ancient concern — it is the context in which every Lauderdale Lakes homeowner should evaluate their current door situation. A standard residential door, even a newer one purchased from a big-box retailer, is not the same as a tested and rated impact door. The difference between the two is the difference between a home that holds its envelope during a major storm and one that suffers catastrophic interior damage from pressure changes and debris penetration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs doors specifically engineered to address the storm profile that Lauderdale Lakes has already demonstrated it can produce.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Lauderdale Lakes and Broward County
section_code.body:
While Lauderdale Lakes is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a special designation that applies to Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — the city is still governed by stringent Florida Building Code requirements that mandate hurricane-rated products for all new door installations and replacements. Under the current Florida Building Code, 8th Edition, any door installed in Lauderdale Lakes must carry the appropriate product approval certification, demonstrating that it has been tested and rated to withstand the 170mph design wind speed assigned to this Broward County wind zone. This is not optional compliance; it is the baseline standard, and unapproved products will fail inspection and must be removed at the homeowner's expense.

Practically speaking, this means that every impact door Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs in Lauderdale Lakes comes with a Florida Product Approval number — a formal state-issued certification that documents the door's performance in large-missile impact testing, cyclic pressure testing, and wind load evaluation. These approval numbers are submitted as part of the permit application to the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division, which reviews the documentation and issues the permit before any installation begins. Following installation, a city inspector visits the property to verify that the work was performed in accordance with the permitted drawings and approved specifications. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step of this process on your behalf.

For homeowners in Oakland Forest or near the Vincent Torres Memorial Park area who are concerned about older homes and non-standard opening sizes, our team has extensive experience working through the technical and administrative requirements of the Lauderdale Lakes permit office. We know what the inspectors look for, we frame our openings correctly the first time, and we don't leave a job until the installation is ready to pass. Proper permitting also protects your home's resale value — unpermitted work can derail a sale and create costly legal complications down the line.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products for Lauderdale Lakes Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing exclusively installs impact doors from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned their place in the South Florida market through rigorous product testing, consistent quality control, and product approval certifications that satisfy the requirements of Florida Building Code in Broward County's 170mph wind zone. When you're investing in your home's protection, the brand on the door matters just as much as the quality of the installation — and both ESW and CWI deliver on every front.

ESW's impact entry doors are engineered with laminated glass panels, heavy-gauge aluminum or steel frames, and multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple locations along the door frame rather than at a single point. This multi-point locking architecture is critical in a hurricane environment because it distributes the load of wind pressure across the entire door frame, dramatically reducing the risk of forced-open failure during storm conditions. For Lauderdale Lakes homes, ESW's product line is available in a wide range of styles — from clean, contemporary designs suited to updated properties to more traditional profiles that complement the older ranch-style homes found throughout Oakland Forest and Lake Emerald.

CWI rounds out our product offering with equally impressive impact-rated sliding glass doors and French doors, giving homeowners flexible options for rear patio access, pool areas, and interior room transitions. CWI's sliding door systems are designed for smooth, low-maintenance operation while maintaining the structural integrity needed to stand up to South Florida's storm season year after year. Both brands offer a variety of finish options, glass tint choices, and low-E glass coatings that help reduce solar heat gain — an important consideration in a city where air conditioning costs are a genuine line item in the monthly budget. Together, ESW and CWI give Lauderdale Lakes homeowners access to a complete, code-compliant impact door solution.

section_cost.h2: What Does an Impact Door Installation Cost in Lauderdale Lakes — And How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Lauderdale Lakes homeowners is a straightforward one: what is this going to cost me? The honest answer depends on a few variables — the type of door being replaced (entry door, sliding glass door, or French door), the size of the opening, the product line selected, and any structural modifications required by the existing framing. For a typical single entry door installation on a Lauderdale Lakes home valued around $250,000, costs generally range from approximately $1,500 to $3,500 per door installed, with larger sliding glass door systems typically ranging from $2,500 to $5,000 or more depending on the configuration. These are real-world ranges based on work we do regularly in this market — not inflated retail estimates.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing understands that the lower-middle income households that make up much of Lauderdale Lakes aren't always in a position to pay for a full door upgrade out of pocket, even when the long-term case for doing so is clear. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified customers. Spreading the cost of impact door installation over a manageable monthly payment makes it possible for families in Oakland Forest and across the city to upgrade their home's protection without waiting years to accumulate the full purchase price upfront. Our team can walk you through your financing options during your free in-home consultation.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors are among the most financially sensible upgrades a Lauderdale Lakes homeowner can make. Beyond the direct insurance savings detailed in the next section, impact doors increase your home's appraised value, reduce energy bills by improving thermal sealing, and eliminate the need to purchase and store accordion shutters or plywood panels each hurricane season. When you factor in those recurring savings and the avoided cost of storm damage to your interior, the net investment in quality impact doors is considerably lower than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premium with Impact Doors in Lauderdale Lakes
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact-rated doors in Lauderdale Lakes is the potential for significant homeowners insurance premium reductions. Florida's insurance market is among the most expensive in the nation, and Broward County homeowners are no exception to that reality. Carriers that write policies in this region — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — are required under Florida law to offer wind mitigation credits to homeowners who can demonstrate that their property meets specific protective standards. Impact-rated doors that have been properly installed, permitted, and inspected are one of the qualifying features that generate those credits.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection, performed by a licensed inspector who evaluates your home's opening protections, roof cover, roof deck attachment, and other structural factors. When your impact doors are documented as meeting the required standards — including the 170mph wind rating applicable to Lauderdale Lakes — that information is reported to your insurance carrier on a standardized OIR-B1-1802 form. Carriers then apply the corresponding credits to your premium, which can translate to savings of anywhere from 10% to 45% depending on the scope of your upgrades and your specific policy. On a Lauderdale Lakes home where annual premiums can easily run $3,000 to $5,000 or more, that's a meaningful dollar amount returning to your household every single year.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing strongly encourages all Lauderdale Lakes customers to schedule a wind mitigation inspection after their door installation is complete and the permit has been finaled. We can recommend qualified inspectors, and we ensure that every installation is documented in a way that makes the inspection process straightforward. The combination of a reduced insurance premium and eliminated shutter costs means that many homeowners find their impact door installation effectively paying for itself over a five-to-ten-year horizon — all while delivering storm protection and peace of mind every single day.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if Lauderdale Lakes isn't right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Lauderdale Lakes sits in Broward County's 170mph wind zone, and distance from the coast does not reduce the wind and debris threats posed by a major hurricane. Storms like Wilma and Irma caused widespread damage well inland from the shore. Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated door products throughout Lauderdale Lakes regardless of coastal proximity, and standard doors — even newer ones — are not rated to withstand those conditions.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in Lauderdale Lakes, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs for a standard entry door installation typically range from $1,500 to $3,500, while sliding glass door systems may range from $2,500 to $5,000 or more depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. We provide free in-home consultations and detailed written estimates so you know exactly what to expect before any commitment is made.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Lauderdale Lakes, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in Lauderdale Lakes require a permit issued by the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division, along with a post-installation inspection to verify code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application process on your behalf, submitting all required product approval documentation and scheduling inspections. You do not need to navigate the permitting process alone — we take care of it as part of every installation.

faqs.q: How long does the installation process take from start to finish?
faqs.a: For most Lauderdale Lakes homes, the physical installation of one to three doors is typically completed in a single day. The broader timeline depends on permit processing time with the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division, which generally runs two to four weeks. Our team will give you a clear schedule during your consultation, and we proactively follow up with the permit office to keep things moving. From signed contract to final inspection, most projects are completed within four to six weeks.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually reduce my homeowners insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurance carriers to provide wind mitigation credits for homes with qualifying opening protections, including properly installed and permitted impact doors rated for Broward County's 170mph wind zone. After your installation is finaled, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your upgrades on the standard OIR-B1-1802 form, which you submit to your carrier. Savings typically range from 10% to 45% on your wind premium, which can represent hundreds to thousands of dollars annually depending on your policy.

cta_headline: Protect Your Lauderdale Lakes Home with Impact Doors from the Kings of Service
cta_body: Schedule your free, no-pressure in-home consultation with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today and discover how easy and affordable it is to upgrade your Lauderdale Lakes home with code-compliant, storm-ready impact doors — backed by the most trusted installation team in South Florida.
